bel: noun. A measure of relative power, defined as log 10 (P 1 /P 2), where P 1 and P 2 are the measured and reference power respectively.
berm: noun. A narrow ledge or shelf, as along the top or bottom of a slope. (Can we verify this sense?)
beseem: verb. Generally with a qualifying word such as ill or well: to appear, look, or seem (a certain way for someone or something).
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.

mel: noun. A unit of pitch on a scale of pitches perceived by listeners to be equally spaced from one another.
melee: noun. A battle fought at close range, one not involving ranged weapons; hand-to-hand combat; brawling.
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.

reb: noun. Clipping of rebel: a Confederate, especially a Confederate soldier.

seem: verb. To appear; to look outwardly; to be perceived as.

beme: noun. A mediaeval trumpet, usually used in hunting or warfare.

merse: noun. Alluvial, often marshy land by the side of a river, estuary or sea.

mrs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maragus.

smee: noun. The pintail, wigeon, pochard, or smew.
